_meta.notes: Annex II = prohibited substances, Annex III = restricted (concentration limits), Annex IV = permitted colorants, Annex V = permitted preservatives, Annex VI = permitted UV filters. Positive-list system for IV/V/VI means unlisted substances are de facto banned in those categories.

formaldehyde.name: Formaldehyde
formaldehyde.notes: Prohibited in cosmetics (Annex II entry 1577). Commonly found illegally in some hair-straightening/keratin treatments.

mercury-compounds.name: Mercury Compounds
mercury-compounds.notes: All mercury compounds prohibited. Historically found in skin-lightening creams and eye makeup from non-EU markets.

lead-compounds.name: Lead Compounds
lead-compounds.notes: Lead acetate and all lead compounds prohibited. Was historically used in hair dyes.

hydroquinone.name: Hydroquinone
hydroquinone.notes: Prohibited as a general cosmetic ingredient. Permitted only in nail products up to 0.02% as antioxidant (oxidative hair dye use only under Annex III).

dibutyl-phthalate.name: Dibutyl Phthalate (DBP)
dibutyl-phthalate.notes: Prohibited since 2004. Was common in nail polishes. Reproductive toxicant (Repr. Cat. 1B).

bisphenol-a.name: Bisphenol A (BPA)
bisphenol-a.notes: Prohibited in all cosmetic products under EU Regulation 2020/1119. Endocrine disruptor.

lilial.name: Lilial (Butylphenyl Methylpropional)
lilial.notes: Prohibited from March 2022 (EU Regulation 2021/1902). Reprotoxic. Was previously common in fragrances, fabric softeners, and leave-on cosmetics.

coal-tar.name: Coal Tar
coal-tar.notes: Coal tar and coal tar dyes not in Annex IV positive colorant list are prohibited.

isopropyl-isobutylparaben.name: Isopropyl & Isobutylparaben
isopropyl-isobutylparaben.notes: Prohibited since 2014 (Commission Regulation 358/2014). Endocrine disruption concerns in leave-on cosmetics applied to child skin.

mineral-oil-unrefined.name: Unrefined/Mildly Refined Mineral Oil
mineral-oil-unrefined.notes: Carcinogenic-grade mineral oils (untreated or mildly treated) prohibited. Highly refined cosmetic-grade mineral oil is permitted.

nitrosamines.name: Nitrosamines / Nitrosating Agents
nitrosamines.notes: Substances that release nitrosamines above detection limits are prohibited. Can occur in products with secondary amines and nitrosating preservatives.

triclosan.name: Triclosan
triclosan.notes: Max 0.3% permitted only in: toothpastes, hand soaps, body washes, deodorants, face powders, blemish concealers. Banned in all other cosmetic product types.

propyl-butylparaben.name: Propyl & Butylparaben
propyl-butylparaben.notes: Max 0.14% for each individual ester, 0.19% combined. Prohibited in leave-on products for children under 3 (nappy area). Endocrine disruption concerns.

resorcinol.name: Resorcinol
resorcinol.notes: Max 0.5% in rinse-off hair dye products only. Must not be used on skin or in leave-on products. Mandatory on-pack warning required.

benzophenone-3.name: Benzophenone-3 (Oxybenzone)
benzophenone-3.notes: Max 6% in sunscreens, 0.5% in other products. Label must declare 'contains oxybenzone'. Potential endocrine disruptor; SCCS raised concerns.

octinoxate.name: Octinoxate (Ethylhexyl Methoxycinnamate)
octinoxate.notes: Max 10% as UV filter. Under ongoing SCCS review for potential endocrine activity.

aluminum-salts.name: Aluminum Salts (Antiperspirant)
aluminum-salts.notes: Max 10.6% aluminum hydroxychloride (as anhydrous aluminum chlorohydrate) in underarm antiperspirants. Spray products must warn against inhalation.

methyl-ethylparaben.name: Methyl & Ethylparaben
methyl-ethylparaben.notes: Permitted up to 0.4% each or 0.8% combined. Broadly considered safe by SCCS but flagged by consumer advocacy groups. Common sensitizers in some individuals.

bht.name: BHT (Butylated Hydroxytoluene)
bht.notes: Permitted as antioxidant at max 0.8%. Some studies associate it with endocrine disruption at higher exposures; SCCS opinion pending full review.

sodium-lauryl-sulfate.name: Sodium Lauryl Sulfate (SLS)
sodium-lauryl-sulfate.notes: Not prohibited, but associated with skin and mucous membrane irritation. No restriction in Annex III, but flagged as a contact sensitizer at high concentrations or with frequent exposure.

dihydroxyacetone.name: Dihydroxyacetone (DHA)
dihydroxyacetone.notes: Permitted in self-tanners up to 10%. Inhalation risk when used in spray form. Spray products must display mandatory warning about inhalation.

styrene.name: Styrene
styrene.notes: Prohibited monomer. Classified as possible carcinogen (IARC Group 2A). May be present as a contaminant in certain polymer-based cosmetic ingredients.
